MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT INDUSTRIAL ELECTRIC SERVICE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Appearances: Richard M. Grant of Brayton Purcell LLP (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party; no other appearances.
This matter was not argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his claims against defendant. By failing to demonstrate with its moving papers that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claims against defendant, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's General Order 129 interrogatory responses are sustained. Interrogatory responses may only be used against the responding party. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Roseanna M. Castillo of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8200), appearing for defendant / moving party Schneider Electric USA, FKA Square D
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Harvey Null

RULING:
DEFENDANT SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC USA, INC. FKA SQUARE D'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to the issue of whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's contention discovery, which identify plaintiff working with Square D products that contained asbestos, preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to the issue of substantial factor causation, defendant sustained its initial burden. Dr. Schonfeld's declaration creates a triable issue whether plaintiff's exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff's asbestos-related illness. Defendant's objections to Dr. Schonfeld's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)
